title: Our Ice Cream Cart — Business Plan

content.title: Our Ice Cream Cart
content.subheading: A tiny business plan for a very cold product

content.title: The opportunity
content.body:
Our town has a **two-kilometre waterfront**, 40,000 summer visitors a week, and exactly zero mobile ice cream vendors.

A single well-placed cart can serve the promenade, the Saturday market, and the evening concert crowd — three rushes a day, one cart, low overhead.

content.title: Projected monthly sales
content.subheading: Scoops sold per month across the season
content.data:
Month,Weekday,Weekend
May,900,1600
Jun,1400,2600
Jul,2100,3800
Aug,2300,4100
Sep,1200,2200

content.title: Unit economics
content.subheading: Per scoop, at our target volume

items.title: Sea Salt Caramel
items.text: Our signature — always on the cart

items.title: Stracciatella
items.text: Fresh cream, shards of dark chocolate

content.title: Rollout plan
content.subheading: From permit to profit in one season

items.title: Permit & cart
items.text: Secure the vendor licence and refurbish the cart.

items.title: Soft launch
items.text: Weekends only on the promenade to tune the menu.

items.title: Full season
items.text: Seven days a week across all three pitches.

items.title: Review
items.text: Close the books, decide on a second cart.

content.quote: The sea salt caramel is genuinely the best thing on this beach, and I have tried everything on this beach.
My kids now navigate the whole promenade by finding your cart. It's a landmark at this point.

content.title: The ask
content.body:
We're raising **€12,000** to cover the cart, the first season's stock, and the vendor permit.

At our projected volumes that capital is returned before the August peak — and by September we're deciding whether cart number two joins the waterfront.

content.title: Let's scoop
content.body: Come find us on the promenade.
